frameworks.name: schema-based memory integration
frameworks.one_line: new memories are integrated with prior knowledge via reinstatement

frameworks.name: reactivation-driven encoding
frameworks.one_line: subjective B-reactivation strength during AC learning supports BC inference

frameworks.name: mPFC-MTL schema network
frameworks.one_line: mPFC and MTL jointly support schema-congruent memory consolidation

core_question: How do prior-knowledge congruency and reactivation interact to support memory integration across indirectly linked associations?
key_assumptions: indirectly linked items can be integrated via a shared cue
subjective reactivation reports index neural reinstatement
manipulations: congruency of BC-association with prior knowledge (congruent vs incongruent)
subjective B-reactivation strength during AC learning
AB-AC inference paradigm with scene (B) and object (C) linked via pseudoword (A)
key_findings: both congruency and reactivation enhanced behavioral memory integration
univariate and multivariate parametric effects in MTL, mPFC, and PPA tracked these behavioral effects
mPFC showed larger functional connectivity with PPA for more congruent associations
interpretations: reactivation and congruency jointly optimize memory integration through reinstatement of prior knowledge
mPFC-PPA coupling implements schema-guided integration of new scene-object links
